behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real STT diarization/model output (speaker-label fidelity), which mocks can hide. This is not just wrapper plumbing. I considered Workflow, but rejected it because the behavior text focuses on STT output preservation itself, not validating multiple model-dependent decisions across a chained pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting audio through the real STT API and verify transcript speaker labels are accurate and usable for attendee attribution in downstream summary inputs.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/structuring logic and can be validated at local function/object boundaries with crafted transcript text. Rejected Integration because the behavior text does not require real infra, cross-module runtime effects, or model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser functions representative transcript snippets and assert assignee/task pairs are extracted into the expected summary-input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Pass/fail depends on what the real LLM generates (coverage/completeness of action items by attendee). Stubbed responses would hide generation failures. Rejected Workflow because this checks one model-output quality condition, not multiple model-dependent steps with cross-step consistency requirements.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcripts with known assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in live structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/orchestration between extraction output and schema mapping. In-process integration (extractor + mapper + schema validator) is the lowest reality check that catches wiring mistakes. Rejected Agentic because behavior text says "identified decisions" (already identified), so the check is routing, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute decision-extractor output through real routing/mapping modules and verify identified decisions land in `decisions` schema fields, not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates model output quality/placement of decision content in structured sections, which requires a real LLM call. Mocked model output would not reveal misclassification or omission. Rejected Workflow because this is a single output-quality assertion, not a multi-agentic-step dependency check.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript inputs through the live summarizer and assert known decisions are present in `decisions` (and absent from being only narrative-only mentions).

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly multi-step sequential processing with cross-step state/merge coherence over repeated model-involved stages (segment STT/summarization then merge). Single-step tests miss failure modes in sequence and merge consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Process a real >60-minute recording end-to-end with real segmentation and sequential segment processing, then verify merged output is coherent and preserves cross-segment action items/decisions/attributions.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end pipeline across multiple stages including real AI/ML calls and externally visible final delivery. It requires validating chained agentic steps and successful autonomous completion, which is beyond single-step Agentic or plain System checks.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting recording through production-like pipeline entrypoint and verify autonomous completion plus delivered structured output artifact via real readback at the external boundary.
